How many of you guys were at Last Funnel Hacking Live? Yeah? So if you're at Last Funnel Hacking Live, the entire event we made, we did the entire theme of the event around this concept called the Lynchpin. Okay, and the Lynchpin is still the greatest thing in the world. I didn't wanna talk about the entire time during this event just because we spent so much time last year. A lot of you guys have seen it, I guess we're at the book. I still believe Lynchpin is the way to grow and scale business faster and better than anything else in the world. We still do it every single day, but after Funnel Hacking Live is done, then we were launching a funnel as a case study for everyone who was going through the two CCX coaching program. And so basically, we would teach the process, we would do it, we would launch it, and people were funnel hacking us and modeling us. And so the first funnel I'm gonna show you guys was basically launched shortly after Funnel Hacking Live, and it is the MFK funnel, okay? And we did a couple of things very interesting on this funnel that were different than we typically do on some of our funnels. The very first one, before I explain this page, the very first page, this is called the reverse squeeze page. I know a lot of you guys have heard of a squeeze page before, right? What a squeeze page typically is for those who are new or to our world and our community. Basically what happens is you have a page and people go to this page, and the only thing you ask on the page is like, hey, would you like some more information about blah? Or would you like a free copy of my ebook or my thing or whatever thing is, right? You have some kind of lead magnet. People come to give you their name and email address, and then on the thank you page, you give them whatever the thing is you promised them, right? And what a reverse squeeze page is, is kind of different. On a squeeze page, you wanna make this page as simple as possible so you get many people to come here to give you the information so that you get as many people as possible going through the funnel this way. With the reverse squeeze page, it's kind of like putting a brick wall in front of it where you're not letting everybody in the door immediately. Instead, you're forcing people to get prepared, get warmed up, and you do something first before you allow them to actually give you their contact information, okay? And so when you look at this page, this is called the reverse squeeze page. So you come right here, see here's the headline. One book really be worth $1.5 million, and it comes down, and they have to actually watch this video before they can do anything, right? There's a video, if they scroll down below to try to do something else, there's literally nothing they can do. It's like, oh, find out how to access this information when you finish watching this video. So when you first open this page, it's like a 15 minute countdown clock, so they gotta wait 15 minutes before they even have the opportunity or the option to put in their email address. And we did this because we had this really cool documentary we had filmed called Biblio Mania. How many of you guys have seen the Biblio Mania documentary? All right, yeah, if you haven't, you should go watch it, it's amazing. We had this documentary, and the first thing we did when we first launched this funnel, is everyone had to go and watch this video, and then after the video was done, then it popped open an opt-in box, it put in their information, then it would take them to the actual MFK page. Do you wanna show them, Ben, what it looks like? After, yeah, so after they watch the video, then it pops up right here, and then they put their contact information, and then it takes them now to the MFK page, okay? So that was the first pages in the funnel here. Look like that. So page number one, reverse squeeze page, video they gotta watch first, after 15 minutes, it gives the ability to send an email address, and then it takes them to the MFK page, and we'll talk about that here in a minute. Now a couple other things to note on this page. One of my favorite things about marketing, if you understand this, it'll change everything for you, but all marketing is, all funnels are really, it's our job in marketing is always to control the frame that we're taking somebody to the next, like the frame that we're taking somebody through, right? And so every single page in a funnel is a frame for the next page, which is the frame for the next page, and so we're trying to control the frame. So if you look at this right here, I know that my end goal is to sell people into a continuity program that's $97 a month, where they get a chance to read old books from people who are dead. And how many of you guys are like, I'm curious actually, right now, how many of you guys spend more than $97 a month on books? Okay, this audience is, you guys are all freaks in here, that's not normal, how about at home? How do you guys spend $90 a day? Okay, yeah, so when you look at that, for most people, they're like, you want me to spend $100 a month to read books from people who, like, I've never heard these authors. These are people who lived a hundred years ago that no one's even heard of anymore. I'm like, yes, exactly. And so the first thing I'm trying to do and I'm in any type of funnel is like, I need to, I'm using story to increase the perceived value of the thing that I'm gonna be selling them in the future. So the goal of this documentary, as I showed this, the goal of it was like, how do we increase the perceived value of the thing I'm gonna offer them later? So we told this story about how I spent $1.5 million on a book. When you first hear that or you see that, you're like, this guy literally is insane. Who would spend a million and a half dollars on a book? I have to watch this, right? Really good hook, we tell a story, and they go through this process, right? What happens is all of a sudden, it starts shifting the value of the information in their brain. Like if Russell's gonna spend $1.5 million for a book, and I get access to it for free inside its membership site, and he's bringing me other books, and also $97 a month seems inexpensive, right? And so it's a pre-frame, it's a frame I'm taking somebody to to increase the perceived value of the thing I'm gonna be offering them later. So that's some of the psychology behind what we did on this very first page. And we launched this funnel the first four days, us and the affiliates, the only thing they could promote directly was this page, right? And they'd go through this page, and from there they'd go to the MFK page, and they could sign up. Before we let anybody actually promote the MFK page. Now I don't have the actual numbers, I couldn't find those last night as I was going through this, but the people who go through and they watch the video first, and then go to the page and sign up, the conversion rate on those people was insane. Most of them, if they watched the 20 minute documentary, and then they signed up, like, it was just, the majority of people bought it, because they'd already invested that much time, they understood it, and the next, the sale on the next page was awesome. So the first four days of the campaign, they had to watch the video before they could go to the MFK page, and then after that, then we started driving traffic directly to the MFK page, okay? So, and what we found over time is, for the reverse squeeze page like this, it works really good on warm traffic and hot traffic, not so well on cold traffic, so cold traffic usually bypasses this page when we're driving traffic as a whole, okay? So that's where the funnel starts. Then page, let's see, is there everything? Yeah, okay, then page number two is the MFK page, we'll pull that up right here. So this page, this is where we're offering a free plus shipping offer, right? They get this thing for free when they sign up for the continuity program. And so if you watch this, we have the headline, we got the sales video right here, we got the two step order for my traditional page. If you scroll down a little bit, you can kinda see what the offer is. So basically the offer, go up a little bit right to the headline, there you go. So whenever I'm creating a free plus shipping offer, like we do in the Lynchpin model, right, when we're doing a MFK, my goal is always to try to figure out how in the world can I make this thing for free, right? I'm not saying, hey, you get this thing, like, you can see the headline, you get all this stuff for free just for saying maybe, right? I'm not saying get this stuff for free when you sign up for the membership site, right? That just feels like a lot of pain, a lot of like, it's just kinda hard, sometimes I make this software like, hey, you get all these gifts for free just for saying maybe, just saying maybe, just try this thing out, right? So if you notice most of my MFK pages, this is the positioning we do, right? So you get all this stuff for free when you say maybe, right? And we gave them a free copy of Think and Grow Rich, gave them a free copy of Applied Psychology, and a free copy of The Secrets of Master Salesmanship, plus you scroll down, we also give them a free copy of the book, the audio book of the book I spent $1.5 million for. So for free, they're getting all these insane things, we made an irresistible offer, they get for free when they say maybe, when they're signing up for a trial to our membership site, we give them a 30-day trial, okay? All right, scroll back up to the top, if you guys wanna look at this page, you can see it later, but that's the first thing. So people go through, they start filling in the first part of the two-step order form, and then let's go to the next step here. So the next step what we started doing is we had a lot of people who didn't wanna pay the printing and shipping for the physical book, right? Especially people overseas who are like, this is a free plus shipping offer, it doesn't cost me $120, that you get shipped out to Africa or to Australia, right? And so we had a lot of resistance to people like, I want this stuff, but I don't wanna pay $150 for you to ship me this thing, like the cost just got too high. And so we decided to offer both options. So there's one option where you get just the membership, you pay a dollar upfront and then you get a fourth-day trial and it rolls in, and you just get access to the membership site and everything digitally, or if you want to ship the box, it's a little bit more, and then they pay obviously the printing and shipping costs of $37 US and international, it's whatever it is, 100 and whatever it ends up being international, right? And so we gave both options, which is really cool. From that, 11% of the people took the digital version, and then 89% of the people took the paid version. So there was a huge segment, 11% of people who, in the past were not signing up because they didn't wanna pay the shipping costs and now they had a chance just to get in. So that's the first thing. Next is you just scroll down, obviously with an order form bump, if you don't have an order form bump on every page you have, you're literally just throwing money away. So the order form bump on this page, there's two things we do, I'm gonna tell you the conversion rates in a minute, but if you notice there's the order form bump right here, where there's an audio set from Napoleon Hill that we sell for $97. And I'm gonna show you something really ninja, we did this funnel here in a second. So just remember, that's what the order form bump looked like on this page, y'all remember this? Okay, don't forget it, cause I'm gonna show you something cool. All right, that was the order form bump, and then, all right, so that was the very first page in the funnel, so someone goes through there, they put in their credit card, they get the things shipped out to them, and then we take them to the first upsell. And the very first upsell page, what we did, is basically, we knew they wanted to sign up for membership, they just signed up for a trial, so the first upsell is basically like, cool, you sign up for a trial, how would you like to get two months for free when you prepay for a year right now? Okay, that's basically the offer. So we go from a trial, a third day trial, upsell is you get a year, you get two months for free, so 10 for 12, 10 months for 12 months, plus we give them an irresistible offer. So I found something really cool in the Napoleon Hill archives, I found this book right here called Hand of Destiny, which was unpublished in the Napoleon Hill book, we republished it, and it's made out of amazing, so it's like, hey, when you sign up right now, and you upgrade, we'll give you a year for free, or sorry, you get a year, you get two months for free, plus we'll give you this bonus, you cannot get anywhere else, the only place you get is right here, right now, and it's $997. So that was the upsell page, and this page converts at, right now, 16.4% of the people who sign up for a trial end up paying us for a year up front. If you don't understand that, that is insane, okay? So 16.5% of the people are upgrading for $1,000 right after they've decided for a free trial. So if you're doing any kind of continuity funnel, you should try that, it works really, really good. Okay, so that's upsell number one. Then we moved to upsell number two, and upsell number two, people only saw if they didn't take the order form bump. So they went to order form, they didn't take it, I'm like, for me, I was like, the order form bump's insane, the offer's so good, so I was like, how could we add this? We add this thing right here, which is basically like, you saw this right here, and for some reason, you said no, are you insane? This offer is so good. So we put that in there, and we give them one last shot to go and to get the order form bump, and actually have a chance now to spend more time selling it. Because initially on order form bump, you have like three sentences to sell, right? So here, as they look back, they're like, if you said no to this, it means you didn't understand it. So I actually show in the video, I show like, here's the videos that we took, and here's the real, we got it transcribed, we had it turned into a physical product, or a digital product, I show the whole thing, tell the story, and then from there, they can buy that. Now again, this is only shown if they said no to the order form bump, and they see this offer, and then they can just say yes, big green button, or no, big red button. And this style of yes, no, with red and green, is converting really, really well, if you guys haven't tested it yet, it converts awesome. So, okay, so that is the OTO that only shows up if they didn't take the order form bump, and then recently we added a third OTO, and so basically what we did is we took another one of these old courses we found and printed, and put it in there as the third spot, and same thing, it's like basically there's this course, super controversial, it's got pulled off, back in the day, the super controversial was pulled off, and we talked about the course, why it's so great, and then from there, basically that two options, they can get a digital version for $297, or we'll ship to the physical copy of it for $397, and right now, if I remember the stats on this, 11% of the people bought the digital, and 89% bought the physical, which is kind of interesting, it's the same numbers we had on the first thing, 11% got the digital on the MIFC, 11% got digital, 11% got digital here, 89% got physical and physical, so people buy digital, buy digital, people buy physical, buy physical, it's kind of an interesting thing. How many of you guys are my physical buyers, you want physical stuff? How many of you guys are my digital people? Okay, is that weird? I remember at one event we did, and this is like the very beginning of ClickFunnels, but Vince James, the very first, I showed you the first one I did at the Broke 2 Comic Club, Vince James book called the 12 Months Millionaire, it's this book, it's like this fat, and we had him printed it for every single person in the inner circle way back in the day, I gave it to everybody, and we got done like a third of the room, literally just left him there, or asked me like, do you have a digital version, I don't really want to carry this thing on the plane, I don't really have a bookshelf, and it was so weird to me, I was like, because I'm obviously a hoarder and I love books, and I have two full offices that rent that just house my books, so for me I couldn't even comprehend that, but there are a segment of people that just want digital, and so by adding that in, it just increases people that can buy it. I think that is the last page in this funnel. So that is the first funnel, the Secrets of Success funnel, again if you look at mapping it out here, this was Bibliomania, so this is the reverse squeeze page, they opt in, from here it takes them to the Mifki page, where they go through, this is where it's free, plus shipping, right, and then from there they got two options, they can get the digital or the physical, there's order form bump for $97, they can get the product, and there it takes an upsell number one, which is the yearly, for $997, and then from there, OTO two, so this is OTO one, for those that at home don't know what OTO, it stands for one time offer, so we have here some say OTO, one time offers, I'm saying, Mifki, so I'm using terminology, I'm sure some of my friends at Back Home may not know, Mifki stands for the most incredible free gift ever, so we make this insane gift, they get for free when they join the membership site, so that's what Mifki is, that's what OTO stands for, OTO two only shows up if they said no right here, right, and that's the $97 OTO, and then OTO three was just another product, we had the digital and the physical version, 297 and 397, so that is the Mifki funnel, we launched this initially and it crushed it, we had a lot of affiliates promote it, it's gone, it's done well, it's put in, I think, I think my exact number's top my head, we put in three or 4,000 people in the continuity program during the very first week, and then that company was in business, and now those people paying $97 a month, and it's awesome, okay, so that is the secrets of success funnel, and I'm tracking right on time, cool, I was like, if I show four funnels, I might go like an hour and a half on each funnel, and then we're gonna be really in trouble, if I have my 15, 20 minutes each funnel, then we're hitting time perfect, okay, so that's secrets of success funnel, do you guys all get at least one or two ideas for things you can tweak on your funnels, from that, is that awesome? Okay, thank you, thank you, Everett Homa, is it been fun, is it good to actually see behind the scenes what all these funnels look like and why we're doing what we're doing? I hope so, okay, it's interesting to me, because I have people all the time, I just don't understand funnels, Russell, I'm like, if you're a body thinker, I'm like, yeah, I'm like, how do you not understand it then, like if you, like, everything I do is funnels, if you just stop hurrying and buy slowly and look at like, what's he doing, why is he doing it, oh, he made an offer here, oh, he had an offer stack, oh, there's an order form bump, like all the things we're talking about, like it's not just theory, you can see it on all my pages, all my places everywhere, in fact, if you go to the school group, I literally have a thread in there, it's like a link to every one of my funnels, so you're like, I'm trying to sell a high ticket thing, how's it work, all the links are in there, you can find it, click on a button, like, oh, there's Russell's funnel, like, it's funnel hack is, right, none of these things that I ever post, like the funnels I'm building, these aren't based on a guess ever, like, I'm obsessed with this game, before we're building a book funnel or a webinar funnel, I will literally go find 20, 30, 40 different funnels in like, like webinar funnels, the book funnels, I look at all of them, we go back through our team, like, we put so much time and effort in every single one, so we look at these things, this is not just guessing, this is like, Russell funnel hacking and geeking out for three months, trying to find the perfect system to build the control, and then we're testing and tweaking, and testing and tweaking as we're going through, so if you look at one of our funnels, it's a pretty good shot that if you start right there, you're probably gonna have something that's gonna work pretty well. So you have permission to funnel hack me. One of the main, like, I feel like my purpose in this community, well, two things, number one, I'm the belief cheerleader, you guys believe in yourselves, that's number one, number two, is I just create funnels and launch them all the time, so you guys can be like, oh, I could do that with my book, I could do a webinar like that, I have a high ticket program, I have a Russell, what if I just did that? And so you guys have permission to model the things I'm doing, because I'm only doing them, so you guys have things to model, because I don't really need to do all this, it's just really fun, and you guys can model me, so. Okay, that's funnel number one. All right, really quick, for those of you guys who understand what the Lynch pin is, I'm not gonna go deep into the Lynch pin, but the way the Lynch pin basically works is you are selling, you have a continuity program, right? We're charging people monthly for something, right? Then you create a MIFKI funnel, a motion credible free gift that you give away to people to get them to join your continuity program. And that's the first two phases of Lynch pin, and then from there, we start focusing on driving traffic to the MIFKI, right? And that's how we launch a business. So again, last year at the Frontline Icon Live, we showed them our Lynch pin model, I said, I'm gonna do what we're talking about, so we created a brand new continuity program called Secrets of Success, we built a MIFKI funnel, we drove traffic, we launched it, boom, we get 3,000 people in here, now I got a business. Then the next phase is like, okay, now how do we start scaling this business? A MIFKI funnel is great for warm traffic, as you start scaling, it gets a little bit harder, because the price is pretty much shipping, and you have upsells and downsells and stuff, but I'm always looking at how do we create something that can spend more money to acquire customers? So the next phase inside of the Lynch pin is we come up here and we create what's called a dramatic demonstration, which a dramatic demonstration is basically a challenge or a webinar or some kind of something that's happening. In fact, by the way, you guys right now, you're all inside of a dramatic demonstration, just so you're all fully aware, okay? Oh, we're in a funnel right now, what is happening? So we do a dramatic demonstration, which is the challenge or a webinar or a live event or something that's exciting, people come to this thing, we serve them, we entertain them, we have a good time with it, and then from there we try to sell something, right? So dramatic demonstration, in dramatic demonstration, we sell whatever it is, but when someone registers for dramatic demonstration, we give them the ability on the thank you page to get the MIFKI, which gets them the continuity, we do the dramatic demonstration, at the end of it we sell them something more expensive, right? So that's basically the gist. So I'm showing you guys this really quickly because the very first funnel I showed you is with the MIFKI funnel. The next thing we did, because I followed the process, because I practiced what I preached, is we did a dramatic demonstration. So I'm gonna show you guys the dramatic demonstration we did for this business, to get people to join continuity, and I'm gonna show you guys how that looks, the stats and numbers behind that funnel. Does that sound good? Okay, so the dramatic demonstration we did for this business, we did the thinking grow rich challenge, and so that was what the next funnel was, okay? And this was fun because we were able to include a whole bunch of just amazing rock stars from the Napoleon Hill world, and Don Green from the Napoleon Hill Foundation, he spoke and a bunch of other cool people spoke, and it was amazing. So yeah, there's a bunch of people who had a chance to speak at it, and it was really cool. I'll tell you guys a funny story in a minute, but I'll not, yeah, okay. So this is the first page of the thing, and it was the beginning of registration page, and you'll notice like there's no rust on here. There's something I learned, I can't remember who I learned it from initially, but there's a really powerful concept in marketing, like if you're trying to get somebody to do something, get somebody to register or get somebody to buy something, like if you just try to do it by, based on your brand alone, it can work really well, but if you can attach it to another brand, it always does better, okay? So for example, when I first got started in this business, eBay was like the coolest thing in the world, like everyone was focused at eBay, right? And so if you had like, how to make money from home, it did well, but if you had how to make money with home on eBay, it just blew up, right? Couple years ago, Amazon became the hot thing, so people had like, how to make money at home, it did well, but how to make money at home with Amazon, you attach it to your own brand, those offers all blew up like crazy, right? Airbnb came out, how to make money that did good, how to make money with Airbnb, boom, it blew up. So it's like, how do you find an existing brand that already is hot, that has market value, things like that, and if you can attach your offer to it, it can explode way bigger, okay? So for me, when I'm doing this, I could have been like, hey, I'm Ryan Drussell, we're gonna read old books together, but no one would have cared, right? Instead, I'm like, what's a known brand? So Think and Grow Rich is obviously a known brand, the book is sold like 100 million plus copies, it's one of the only personal-developed books that I think most people on the planet, at least in our world all know what it is, but it's a known brand, and so we attach this challenge to that brand, okay? We did something similar when we launched ClickFunce 2.0, it could have been like Russell, like hey, this is Russell and ClickFunce, and there's like Russell's the brand, ClickFunce is the brand, but I was like, what's another brand I can attach it to to help this grow? And so the brand we attached to ClickFunce 2.0 launched to was Damon John, I'm like, Damon is a brand, he's bigger than me, he's on TV every single week, people know who he is, and so we attached Damon John to it, which makes conversions increase, okay? I remember one of my friends, Chet Holmes, before he passed away, Chet was running these radio ads forever, and he was like running a really profitable, really solid business radio ads, eventually part of Tony Robbins, and he had Tony just like, hey, my name's Tony Robbins, I want to use my friend Chet Holmes, and then Chet would do the rest of the radio ads, and by having Tony's brand intro the ad that Chet ran, their ad cost dropped down to a fourth, just by attaching it to a brand, okay? So when we launched the Think Growers Challenge, you can ask John about this, who rides all of our traffic, John's like, this is the cheapest leads we have ever had ever, I was like, yeah, this is amazing, so if you want to get lower ad costs, start thinking about these things, part of it with Damon and John, our ad costs dropped dramatically, right? So thinking about this, how can you attach yourself to some other brand that's a known entity that you can drop ad costs with? So Think Growers is who we did the challenge with, and again worked in the Poling Hill Foundation to get the ability to co-brand with that. This registration page, Convert-A-Well, all right, next page, so it's a free challenge, next page then is the thank you page, and this is where basically, according to the Lynch pin, what we do here is we get people to join a continuity program, and so this is basically the VIP offer, we did this one different, in the past we've done where like, you get VIP for free when you sign up for the continuity program, this time we actually charged for it, in part of a test, and so we basically say, hey, you can VIP for $47 right now, and we're gonna give you a 38 trial to the Secrets of Success Challenge, or sorry, Secrets of Success Membership site, and so that was kind of the price point there, $47 to get VIP for free when you join the continuity program, we'll ship you out, everything from the MFKEE. This page right here, we convert it 3.84%, so other people who registered for the challenge, 3.84% signed up for the continuity program, they paid $47 upfront, and they got on trial for $97 a month. Now this is actually lower than we typically like, for us, if we can get anywhere from like, 5% to 8% of the people on this page to sign up for the MFKEE, like that's the sweet spot of the numbers that works, and this is actually kind of a fun stat to share, because we were in the inner circle, I did this thing called Deccana Day, where everyone comes to Boise, I can't remember who it was, but they came out, oh yeah, they came to Boise and they're like, here's our MFKEE, we launched it, didn't really work in the challenge, I'm like, what did it convert to that? What was it, was it 5% to 6%? Yeah, it was like 5% to 6%, they had only 5% of people upgraded on the thank you page, so I was like, that's the number we're targeting, we have 5%, this is a multi-million dollar funnel, they're like, oh, seriously, oh crap, we didn't know we were doing the right thing, I guess the numbers are great, now just scale it, go for it, go, go, go, go, and so anyway, this one was a little low, 3.4%, I would like ideally to double that, so that's something we're working on right now is increasing conversions, but there's the order form bump, oh sorry, that was the upfront, and then down here we had an order form bump, which obviously is the same order form bump that we had on the MFKEE funnel, so we didn't have to come up with something new, it's the same offer, same order form bump, this on the challenge page, it converted at 6.13%, no sorry, wrong number, 38.09% upgrade to the order form bump right here, and then on the next page, can you guess what happens on the next page? After I sign for this, then it takes them directly on the next page to a year worth of click funnel, or sorry, not click funnel, you get a year worth of secrets to success, you're two months for free, and we give this cool book when you upgrade right now, so same up, self-flow, I didn't have to make up a new funnel, it's just, we just plugged it into the challenge and same funnel metrics, the conversion rates on the page here, people buying the yearly, a little lower on this one, it's 6.13% of the book, sign for trial, sign it for the yearly, okay. All right, now I'm gonna walk through what the actual challenge looked like, so we did a three day challenge, for various reasons, there's a lot of different challenge structures, some of you with three days, some of you with five days, this one we wanna do a three day, and so the very first day of the challenge, so what happens, we train for like 90 minutes, I talked about something, we had Napoleon Hill come watch the video, we had guest speakers come in, so it was 90 minute training basically, and then after that was done, then we had VIP, basically VIP access, and so what's cool about this, VIP is the thing that people bought, when they registered right, they get VIP, what happens is the very first day training starts happening, people show up, you do the first day live training, at the end of it, you're like, all right, all my VIPs, we're gonna get started in 15 minutes, it's gonna be amazing, we're gonna talk about, it's gonna be awesome, and if you're not a VIP yet, go sign up right now, and so we had an extra, by doing that, 187 people who were live right here, who weren't VIPs, went and signed up for VIP, 187 there, 17 of them, signed up for the yearly upgrade, and I can't remember my stats, three did something awesome, I can't remember what it was, something awesome, yeah, don't remember, anyway, must not have been that important, okay, so this is day number one, now what's funny is, in the VIP we brought in different trainers to come and train, people who knew Napoleon Hill, and we're kind of obsessed with him, how many of you guys are really thinking rich, by the way? You know there's like one chapter, it's kind of like, huh, yeah, you know what I'm talking about? So those are chapters, as I'm planning to think gross challenge, I'm like, we're gonna talk about a whole bunch of Napoleon Hill principles, but we're definitely gonna skip the chapter on transmutation of sexual energy, because I don't wanna talk about that with anybody, let alone a whole bunch of people in the challenge, right, so I'm like, we are not talking about that, let's just pretend like that chapter's not even in here, but then we were messaging some of the guest speakers, and John Gray, manor from ours, womanor from Venus, that guy, he loves Napoleon Hill, he's like, can I be on your challenge? We're like, that'd be great, like this would be awesome. So we made him one of our VIP guests, and we told him we had 20 minutes to talk about his favorite Napoleon Hill principle, and we told them that there were gonna be three speakers over the next hour, but what he heard was, I have an hour, and so we opened it up, and we're like, hey John, and so I was like, I have some questions to ask you, so I had some questions pre-set up, I was gonna ask him to lead him, where I wanted him to go, and we literally mean Justin Benton, is Justin in here? And that still works when I do live webinars really, really well, but when we started evergreening them, we had kind of an issue because it was harder to get people to show up to an evergreen webinar. So let me show you what I mean by that. So when I do webinar live, I come, they register for the webinar, okay? Thank you page, we put people into a trial, then we do the actual webinar, and then we'd sell something here at the end, right? That's the traditional webinar funnel. Now when we were doing evergreen, we do the same thing, come on, someone go here, we get people on a trial, but then the show up rate to evergreens, just for whatever reason, are always way, way, way less, right? And so it made it hard because we do this whole campaign, we get people on here, but then the show up rate, for these auto webinars, sometimes we're like 10%, 8%, we're trying to alter things like, how do we get more people? If we got more people to actually buy here, we could increase how much money we were doing, and it was just, we were testing a thousand different things, and it was just harder and harder and harder, and what we started doing is like, okay, what if we switch the way we do, what if we shift the way we're thinking about this whole thing as a whole? What if instead of immediately someone registers, and we're trying to get them on continuity right now, what if we delay this process and move it to a different part of the funnel? Okay, so for evergreening funnels, we shift where the continuity program is happening. That makes sense for the more advanced people. So what happened is someone would come in, they'd register, as soon as they register, immediately put in the webinar, as long as as many people as possible seeing the live event, right? So they'd register immediately to see the webinar, they'd get jumped in there, and right now we're getting like 89 to 90% of people to start the webinar, right? So that's like, okay, wait until we get in there, now I can grab their attention if I have a good hook, and we start taking them through the webinar, and then we have the whole sequence to get them to buy on the webinar, we have our car closing, urgency, scarcity, car closes, and then when the campaign ends at the end of it, then when it's over, then we do what's called a no customer left behind campaign. I think the person that does this the best is actually Tony Robbins. If you're on any of his challenges, you'll see what he does. He goes through the whole challenge, it's amazing, everyone experiences it, car closes, and the next day it's like, hey, if you weren't able to buy this, we don't want you to feel left behind, we don't want you to lose your momentum and your progress, and so what we do is have a really cool program you can jump in right now, it's really cheap, it's inexpensive versus the $2,000 thing you didn't buy, or the $5,000 thing you didn't buy. When I first launched, we launched the .com Secrets Book. Man, when was that? Ten years ago when we first launched it. Order form bumps weren't a thing then. There were people doing it, but it was really hard to custom code them before ClickFunnels came. We were the first platform that had order form bumps. You could just click a button and they were in there. When I launched the .com Secrets Book, it's the very first funnel I actually built by myself, launched on ClickFunnels platform, and the order form bump converted at 60-something percent. It was crazy. Then everyone started using order form bumps, and the conversion rate across the board, at least most of my funnels, order form bumps, conversion dropped, and they dropped and dropped to now. Usually, 20 to 30 percent is kind of where usually it settles in on order form bumps. This order form bump, for whatever reason, I don't know if it was the hook, the angle, the whatever, this order form bump right here, maybe it's because it's so congruent. To the point where we ended up with 144 people, paid the $297 to get the question answered. If you give them three minutes, you're at like 12 hours a day. So that was the problem. Your first job is to get somebody else to do the One Fun Away Challenge, and we'll pay you 100% affiliate commission for doing that. And so this page was the same thing basically. There's a video of me like, welcome to challenge. I hadn't been in school groups, we were going to communicate in school with them, and scrolling a little further, is we gave them the chance to be in affiliate. I said, look, you refer somebody, and you get 100%, so you get your money back instantly, refer two people, you double your profits, and every person after that you refer, it's all just free money. And we did something cool that I'm going to share with you guys in a minute. It's called our instant affiliate links. As soon as they're registered, by default, they have an affiliate link ready to rock and roll, they copy and paste that, and they can start promoting it right out of the gate. What's cool about that is people are joining, and then they're referring the friends, and they're referring the friends, and it started growing without me doing anything. So yes, we paid 100% ticket price for the $100 ticket, and then we paid 40% of all the other things inside of the upsell flow. And so again, every person signed up, it kept growing because the 100% affiliates kept promoting, kept promoting. Okay, a couple things. But basically, three features I'm going to talk about. I'm going to talk about the strategy behind the feature. And then Todd's going to show you guys actually how it works instead of click funnels. So, the first feature we're going to talk about, this one element that we have inside ClickFunnels now could literally double your conversion rates or more just by understanding how to actually use it. Okay? And so, I'm going to talk about the strategy behind how to use it. Basically, the element, well, I should let it with what it is, but it is the new survey quiz element, which is really cool. And we'll talk about the strategy behind this work. So, typically, when we're driving traffic, like, a lot of times we're driving traffic to a funnel, someone's coming there and we're hoping, okay, we have 100,000 people this year that come to this funnel. We're hoping to like, crash a big enough net and a broad enough message that everyone's going to be able to converse as many people as possible, right? But it's hard because so many different people are coming to you. And so, I remember the very first time we did this, this is a few years ago, we sat down and we were like, who are all the people that actually use ClickFunnels? We were like, well, there are people that want e-commerce. There are people who want it because they want it in an agency. There are people that are affiliate markers. There are people that do info products. There are people that do network marketing. They're all different. And honestly, the way I would teach each of these people how to use the platform is completely different, right? And so, we're trying to cast this wide net and everyone's just confused. And it's like, hey, what if we created a survey that helped us figure out exactly who our customers were and then based on that, we just talked to them based on exactly who they are, right? And so, this was the old ClickFunnels homepage. We called this the mother funnel because it was so much work to put together. But it was kind of cool. So, you come here and it's like, hey, I've given us, like, take this short quiz to find out which of these funnels work the best for your specific type of business. And it's like, step number one, what kind of, what describes your business the best, right? And they have these different options. So, they would tell us exactly what type of business they had. And then from there, we'd go and we'd ask a bunch of other questions, right? Different things, right? How much money are you making? How many leads did you get? We asked a handful of different questions to get to know exactly who they were. And by the time they were done with that survey, we knew, okay, they're one of these 10 buckets. They're either this kind of person, this kind of person, this kind of person, right? So, from there, we would then take them to, we had this little gift come up like, a case study is being generated for you. Boom, it would pop out. Okay. And then based on which kind of person, which kind of avatar they fit into, we had different things. So, they were network marketers. They saw Mindy and Mandy's case study here. If they were into their local business, they saw Yonny's. If they were doing info products, they saw Natalie's. So, they had, we had 10 different versions, right? So, we come here to the case study page. They then put the email address in. If you get the case study for someone who had the exact same type of business they had. Then from there, we'd take them to a page that would basically have a video of someone who had the exact type of business, teach them exactly how to use funnels, and then have cells there down below to sell and convert that person perfectly. Okay. So, what happened is we used this survey funnel to go and take all the world of traffic, right? We put all these people down, and then we were able to take each person into the exact bucket of type of business they had. And then from there, we had a sales video, sales message, and case study that spoke specifically to them. Okay. So, my question for you guys is, how many of you guys at home have a product that fits more than one avatar, right? There's two people, or three people, or five people who could buy your product. Okay. That's how this shifts everything because it gives you the ability to start communicating to your audience based on who they are, not just based on traffic as a whole. Okay. So, number one is our survey quiz element. And Pastor Todd has kind of shown us a little bit about what this element does. Yeah, absolutely. I mean, this element is almost, I mean, it is more than an element, right? Like, this is an app. This is the entire survey experience. There are many products on the market that do nothing but this one thing. I was gonna say, there's competitors that charge $97 a month just for this one feature, and this app actually does it better, I believe. And going back to the price point on ClickFunnels, it's free for all of you guys in your accounts. No extra fees, right? We're constantly gonna be over delivering, constantly rolling out new apps for you guys. So we took the best practices that we learned, right? So we had these strategies that we implemented on our own stuff. We've done plenty of surveys over the years. You remember we did the dot-com secrets, basically our split test, 108 split test book back in the day. One of the top results that we got from that was related to surveys. And I'll actually throw that in as like a pointer and a tip too here in a second. But if we go through, you'll see when you get into ClickFunnels, if you haven't been into the new platform, you have what we basically phrase as an app store. These are not paid third-party apps. These are apps we built for you guys inside of ClickFunnels. You just click a button and set them up. They're all separate to keep from overwhelming you, so the user experience is nice and clean and simple. You just jump in, click Survey Workflows, and start creating your first workflow. This is what it can look like as you build out a new workflow, though. And we took every feature request that we got from Russell, that we got from you guys as beta testers on this. Some of you guys have been beta testing this for months. We took all of that feedback, and it's all implemented as of today. So this is just an example funnel that we built out, and I'll kind of walk you through how we set it up here. So we start out by checking your email address, grabbing your email address, asking some bucketing questions, as Russell called it. The idea there is that we're going to ask you a few questions. We're going to walk you through it. We're going to get to know you. But there's a specific question or two in the funnel that you're actually going to use to determine the outcome of the survey, whether you're going to send it somewhere, submit the form, redirect them, send them straight to your sales guy. Those kind of questions can be used to determine that. And we actually allow unlimited bucketing rules with this. So you can just go nuts. There's no restrictions on that. Can't wait to see what you guys actually come up with, too. So start with the Survey Workflows app. You come in. You just simply add questions and splits. Very basic, very simple to use. Your questions are obvious. You're going to come in here. You can add multiple choice questions. You can add input questions, so free form input questions. If you're doing a free form input question, or actually multiple choices as well, you can actually assign it to a contact attribute. So inside a click funnel, you can basically come in here. You could decide gender. You could decide business size. You could decide industry, whatever that is. And it stores on that context record permanently. So at least unless they change it, it's in stored on their record. So you can use that information in follow-up funnels later. You can use it in redirects. You can use it in the workflow. So it's something that's basically on them indefinitely. So you can come in here. You can set up inputs. You can set up multiple. You can decide if they can select multiple, checkbox, radio style, like all that's possible inside of it. The next level down is basically creating the splits. So this is using the information that happened on the previous step to do one of two different paths down the funnel. So you could go left. You could go right. If it's a yes condition, you're going to go right. And you're going to land on the next question. So here you can see an example of adding a question, editing rules. Here's an example of what you can set up for a rule. What your current revenue was asked on a previous step. So we're going to use that. If it's equal to 50,000 plus, we're going to send them down one path. If they're equal to getting dedicated support, if they've reached out and said they want a specific path, then we're going to send them down that path. So this is what it ends up looking like in this example. One redirect could go to your sales team. One redirect could go to a free sign up page. Just depends on the value of the customer. We're able to customize that whole experience. And it makes what took us forever back in the day on the mother funnel to build is actually just natively built into ClickFunnels now and we could build it an afternoon probably. Yeah. So that's what SurveyFunnels look like. Now there's two parts to SurveyFunnels. You have the actual back end logic of the SurveyFunnel. You've got a cool user interface that we're looking at, looks similar to our funnel builder, has a very graphic visual style. And then you have the actual element itself. The element itself you're going to actually put on any page. One of my favorite parts about it personally is you can build this survey once and you can put the element on 50 different pages and just select the back end logic that the element will use. So you don't have to go rebuild this thing over and over and over again after you invest in that logic. So you put the element on there. It presents different styling options, different customizations for colors and fonts and all the things you would want to customize on the element using our best practices again that we had tested previously. Those are the defaults. And the guys are working on constantly adding new UI options for this element too. We've got a roadmap of all kinds of awesome stuff coming. But you can already do all the things that we've used as best practices for years. So that's basically what the element looks like. And if we go, sorry, to the next one here. I'm going to show an example just of it playing here. I don't know if I need to press play again. There it is. Cool. So this is just an example of walking through a survey, seeing the data go in automatically, you know, collecting the email address, collecting all the data. Again, all the colors here, the fonts, the button styles, all this is customizable. So it's obviously going to be on brand for you because it's all built inside of our page editor. And all this data that's being collected gets to get stored on the contact record. So again, you could use it in workflows, follow-up emails, any conditional stuff. You could use it in your cells pipelines to categorize which pipelines people are going to go to. Just the nature of ClickFunnels 2.0 having all of this in one place allows you to just use this everywhere automatically. There's no integrations. There's nothing to connect. There's nothing to do there. It just does what you would hope. That's it. That's the survey feature. All right. Have you said about that? In the chat, if you guys are talking about a survey feature, blow it up, let Todd know how happy you are for him. It's great news for you guys. Put it in the chat everywhere. I will see it. All right. That's the first thing. Next cool feature is the game changer. I want to talk about this concept. How many guys know the power of affiliates? Okay? We didn't buy our first Facebook ad until ClickFunnels has been live almost like two years. Part of that, all of our entire growth initially came from affiliates, from joint venture partners. We found people that had our dream customers, they promoted our webinars, and that's how ClickFunnels grew out of the gate. After like two years, we should probably learn how to run ads to grow our company. We had nothing. And then in the team, we were like, we want to learn how to run ads, and John raised his hand and like, cool, you're the ad guy. Go figure it out. But prior to that, everything we built was off of this. Prior to ClickFunnels, the entire decade of my business prior was 100% driven by affiliate traffic. And for some reason, we never talk about that in this community anymore. Everyone's talking about Facebook ads, and Google ads, and Instagram. All that stuff is great, but it was crazy a decade ago. Prior to this whole like, nowadays, like we come to events like this, all we ever talked about were joint ventures. How do we get people to promote? Like that was how we got traffic back in the day. And it was better because I think it's better because when you get affiliates to promote something for you, they drive traffic, they take on the risk, they make a sale, and then you only pay them if they make you money. It's not like Zuckerberg. Zuckerberg is like the worst affiliate of all time. He's like, hey, pay me up front, and I'm going to send you some good people, some bad people, and if you don't make any money, it doesn't really matter to me. So go for it. Oh, and put a pixel on your landing page that way. If you are making some profit, I can suck that out by raising that price because I know exactly how much you're making. And he's the worst affiliate of all. Right? I talk about building and dreaming. In fact, my next slide is like, you're Dream 100, right? Dream 100 strategy is like finding the people that already have your dream customers on their list. And most of you guys' businesses run with one Dream 100, just Mark Zuckerberg. And he is the worst partner of all the partners. He does not want you to win. He just wants your money, and he's really good at getting it from us, right? So I want you to understand, like, in our community, we'll talk more and more about this because it's like such a game changer when you start understanding it by getting other people to become your affiliates. You become affiliates of other people's products, and one of the best ways to grow your company without any risk, without any stress, without any headaches. Okay? And so I'm going to teach you real quick a faster review of how we get affiliates. So the concept I talk about in the Traffic Secrets book is the Dream 100. And basically, the Dream 100 is like, who already has my dream customers figured out? Right? So what I do is I make a list like this. The left-hand list is Facebook. I'm like, who are all the people that have big Facebook followings of my dream with my dream customers, right? So we launch ClickFunnels. I did this. I went to Facebook. I'm like, okay, Tony Robbins has 3.2 million followers, right? Grant Cardone had like 1.8 million followers. And I started going through, who are all the people that already have my dream customers on Facebook? I made a huge list of them. They went to Instagram. Who are the Instagram influencers who already have my dream customers? I went through and made a list of 100 of those people. They went, who are the podcasters? I went to the podcast directory. Who are all the people who already have podcasts who have my dream customers listening to them? And we went channel by channel by channel to the point where we had my Dream 100 list that was like Dream 800 or something like that, right? And then when we started promoting our products, right, when we launched ClickFunnels, the very first thing we did, this is before video cards were a cool thing. We called Hong Kong. We found it. You can make these video cards. And I recorded 800 videos of me to every single one of these persons saying, hey, my name is Russell Brunson. I love your YouTube following. I love your YouTube channel. We have a new product come out called ClickFunnels. And I made a customized video for 800 plus people. We put them on these little things and we shipped out these things to 800 different people to go and talk about that. When the DotCom Secrets book came out, first thing we did, built a Dream 100 list. I sent a free copy of that book to 800 different people and then I call them all on the phone trying to get them to promote. And this is the power of the Dream 100. You don't have to get all 800 people, all 100 people to say yes. You just get one. It can change your life forever. I remember the DotCom Secrets book. I sent all these different packages to my books and most people didn't respond back. Most people didn't respond to anything. And all of a sudden one day I got a message from John Lee Dumas. I'd never heard the guy before. I didn't really know. I knew he had a podcast. We'd never talked prior. But he messaged me, hey, Brunson, I got your book. It's really good. I said, thanks, man. He's like, can I put you on the podcast and interview and talk about the book? I was like, that'd be amazing. He's like, do you mind if I email about this book as well to my entire customer? I said, that would be fantastic. We have an affiliate program set up for that. So John became an affiliate, put me on the podcast. He said, I think these are like 30 emails to his list. Every day for a month. I didn't ask for that. I was super grateful for it. And he ended up selling like over a thousand copies of my book based on one person saying yes. So it was the only one from the podcast list. I probably had, I don't know, eight or nine people from the podcast list who said yes. And they got my on their podcast and their podcast and their podcast. And then four or five people from the Facebook list put me on their channels and four or five people sent email to their list. And based on all that's how we drove traffic in all of our businesses. Okay. So when you're thinking about traffic a little bit differently, instead of just focusing on buying ads from Mark Zuckerberg and things like that, it's like, who are the people that already have your dream customers you can do partnerships with. You bring them in and you can give them sales on your products. Right? Okay. That's the first thing on the show. A couple other things that are cool about affiliate programs. A lot of times one of the other cool features is like we always build an affiliate center anytime we're in an affiliate program. So when we launched one Funaway Challenge, we had an affiliate center where they had swipe files and ads and emails and things they could do so they could log in and they have a bunch of examples they could use to make it easier to promote for affiliates. Okay. So these are some of the things that we were thinking about as we built out the new affiliate app which in the past in ClickFunds class we call this backpack, but this is such a huge update and big change. We're just calling it the affiliate app and this is a huge game changer. We've been using this now for about a year in the ClickFunds side of the business. It's been battle tested. It's amazing. And you want to talk about what's happening with the affiliate app. Absolutely. So that's a really good point. We've been using this for well over a year now. We've been battle testing it. It's been the most requested feature on our feedback boards for probably two years, right? So we haven't been ignoring you. We've built it and we've been using it. This is one of those critical pieces. We're dealing with money not just being collected but also being distributed and charge backs and claw backs and all the things and complexities that come with accounting ledgers and stuff when you have multiple parties transacting back and forth. So the point is we spent a lot of time getting it right, making sure we had easy ways for you guys to look into how commissions happen so your support teams are able to actually service people when issues come up. The industry as a whole has been with affiliates in my opinion, has had either really complex confusing lower quality options or really, really expensive high end options that just aren't viable for most people to use. So we decided we wanted to build in the capabilities and the features and the security of the highest end options but included for free inside of ClickFunnels for everyone at the 97 price point which is also way less than it was in Classic as well. So just to point that out for you guys. So we'll walk through the affiliates app real quick. All of that's in there for you guys to set up your affiliate program in the affiliate center. All right. You talk about this hack because this is awesome. This is like the Russell request. We were launching the selling online challenge. I was like, I want to get the affiliate to 100% commission. He's like, that's really easy to do. I'm like, but I don't want them to have to go sign up for the affiliate program because then there's resistance, right? What if we set up, like someone registers for selling online challenge and on the thank you page, we just give them an affiliate link and they can just have it. They start promoting rather gate and they start promoting and sales are coming in. Then when they want to go collect the commissions, they can log in and add all the rest of the stuff, but they can be going to fill it instantly immediately. We did it in the selling online challenge, but you can do it anywhere where somebody registers. They opt in for a free lead magnet. They keep aging by congratulations. Here's your filling to go promote this and you make affiliates happen on the fly. In fact, I won't go too deep on this, but in the traffic seekers book, I talk about this concept called the viral coefficient, which I learned from Mark Joyner, who's my first mentor in the internet game. He talked about if you want something to go viral, right? If you have a site and you drive traffic, you get one person to sign up. If that person signs up, nobody, which normally happens is not viral, right? You have to pay for every single person coming in. But if someone comes in and for every time someone signs up, if they get one person to sign up, it's not viral, but now every time someone comes in, there's one more person and it creates a flat line. But if every time someone signs up, if they get 1.1 person, all of a sudden starts growing and it creates this viral coefficient, right? So imagine when you have a landing page or a challenge or a webinar or something and you start incentivizing people to refer and if you get something that's actually viral, every time you see it's something to register for a webinar, that turns into three registrations or five registrations or whatever that thing might be. Also, your ad cost is dropping down because yes, you're paying for that one registration, but then you get three people to register because it's viral coefficient. And so we're always saying, like, how do we create things to have viral coefficients greater than one, right? 1.1, 1.2, 1.5, 2.0? They ask how things start growing virally. And so in the past, it's hard to do that because someone signs up and they keep you coming to fill it and then they have to go somewhere else and they create an account and then they got to put it in their social security number and they send you like their W2s and it's this whole nightmare to get somebody to be in affiliate. Now if you follow this process, literally they become an affiliate instantly on the fly, they can start promoting and then when they want to get paid, then they have to go do stuff, but all the friction is gone on the front end so you can get people in immediately, which is insane. Okay? So we tested initially on the Song of the Line Challenge. This is how it worked. Someone signs up, boom. And then the next page, they pay the money and over here, I'm like, okay, your first step right now is to promote it and boom, there's a filling. They can grab it instantly, start promoting it and they start earning commissions instantly, right out of the gate. They don't have to do anything else. They start promoting it. Commissions are coming in since they want to get those commissions out, then they log in their account, submit all this stuff and then you start paying them out. But that way you can start getting people to start promoting your things very, very quickly. Is that amazing? That's... Thank you, Todd. You'll notice like... These guys all understand. Yes. What's crazy about it, you'll notice this... In our paid challenge, it doesn't have to be a paid challenge. You can do it anywhere. Every opt-in, every registration, you can do it anywhere you want. And so there will basically be a framework where we'll give you guys post-event where you take this framework, you plug in your fillet, the system that gives you the entire process, add something like this into it and then you guys can do what we're doing and make your things grow a lot more viral. This is pre-click funds. This is probably about 12, 13 years ago. I was in Kenya and we were building schools with Stu and Amy and a whole bunch of stuff with the Bill Jimpack. They're not that talented, but they plugged into a level 10 opportunity and someone sold their companies for billions of dollars. He's like, I look at myself, I think I have a level 10 skill set, but I'm in a level two opportunity. So I can't get past this scene no matter how hard I try. He's like, dude, you're one of the most talented guys I know. If you just applied what you do to the right business, you can make a lot more money. And by the time we were at TNC and I come back to Todd, I'm like, what's our level 10 opportunity? Like, I don't even know. And I think I told you yesterday, our big idea we had is like, we're going to create this thing called WP undies. And that's going to be our thing. And like, we started these things and we tried for a long, long time. And eventually, obviously for us, we went from what we were doing, our level two opportunities, we were launching funnel after funnel, which was great. Our level 10 opportunities shifted. We went from us doing funnels to us showing the world how funnels work, us creating software for the world, us doing the training and coaching on what we were doing inside of funnels. That was our big level 10 opportunity that we shifted into that built what we do right now. I shared the same concept, same principle with this guy right here. How many years ago, Alex Armosi? But then he started shifting from the level two opportunity to level 10 opportunity, teaching other school owners how to grow their businesses and blew up their entire business as well, right? When we bought Dan Kennedy's company, I went through and I found, I don't know, probably 50 different people who had the same thing. Every business, like this guy, Paul was a physical therapist, Ben Glass was a lawyer, we had another lawyer, a fitness coach, chiropractor and all these guys basically became the best in their businesses, right? Every business you can dream of, like you can kind of, all of them are corporate bankers, pastors, opticians, dog walkers, like they were all doing the business, right? They learned from Dan Kennedy, learned how to do marketing and then they shifted and moved from a level two opportunity to a level 10 opportunity, helping people inside their businesses and they all started blowing up, focusing on a level 10 opportunity, okay? So why am I sharing all this, right? The big secret, if you want to transform, the big secret is you can transform your life experiences into wealth, control and freedom, like that's the big a-ha and that's what frameworks allows you to do, okay? So for all of you guys now who have businesses and you are doing this process for your businesses, right? That is great, like using funnels to grow your company, but the bigger opportunity for most of you guys is like, how do we take this and help other people in our industry do the same kind of thing? And so literally what you can do with frameworks is you can take all of the marketing, the funnels, the traffic, the follow-up sequences, the emails, the sequence, all that kind of stuff and you can build it into a business and then you can share that entire thing with somebody else, okay? I always talk about this like, what frameworks are is basically it's like share funnels on steroids. Like share funnels, we launched that, it was huge because everyone could have created a funnel, they could share it with other people, which was great, right? But a funnel is not typically your entire business, right? You have your funnel, you have your CRR, you have your membership site, you have your email sequences, things like that. You can now take all those things, package them together and then you can give that or you can sell that to people and change their business and change lives as well, okay? I have a friend who literally, he's all he does is he licenses stuff and he has a six email sequence that he wrote for chiropractors that they send these six emails to their clients, it gets more clients to come back in and he licenses the email sequence. He goes to people, to chiropractors and they write him a check up front to license it and he gets a percentage of the sales, they send these six emails and then he takes a percentage of the money from it. It has a huge multimillion dollar business based on licensing six emails he wrote. That's it. The second one time he's like, hey Russell, I wrote a bunch of emails. If you send these emails to your list, selling your info product, I'll license these emails to you for X percent. Like it's crazy, but he's just basically took a framework, six emails and he has a multimillion dollar business like licensing those emails to other people. So there's so many different opportunities you have to do with the stuff that you're already doing, the funnels, email sequences, the membership sites, all kinds of stuff where you can create something and then you can share the entire business to people and that's really the level 10 opportunity we're talking about. I think I'm going to pass it to you now. Oh yeah. So originally we invented share funnels where you can basically share a funnel. What's cool about this is again, it's the next level. It's like share funnels on steroids. We can share your entire business, your entire platform and like that. So we'll talk about frameworks. Yeah, absolutely. Yeah. The frameworks, there's two different sides to it, right? There's the claiming side. There's the side of actually being able to take someone else's framework and apply it into your workspace. That could be a starting point for an entirely new business. When I say workspace, business, same thing, right? It could be a starting point for an entire new business or it could be stacking something else on. Russell for example is building a number of frameworks and you're going to be cranking out new frameworks for people. I can't wait to see all this. But Russell's going to be cranking out new frameworks that you'll be able to apply on top of your existing businesses, right? So there's the claiming side of things, making it easier, faster, simpler to get started for you guys online. But when it comes to stepping into this level 10 opportunity side of things, there's creating those frameworks, right? So this is actually the simple process and it literally is a simple process for creating a framework. This is the experience that somebody receives as the framework is actually being installed, right? It's just telling them, hey, these things are coming into your account. It's installing all the stuff. But the actual experience for building a framework is simply you building the workspace, building the business, build the funnels, build the workflows, build the stores, build the products, build the setup that you want to give to other people. And then you're going to be able to just run through the actual installation of the framework. Before that, you want to jump into Pedro's? Yeah. Are you sure? So with Pedro, Pedro did a challenge a month ago with us. And somebody has maybe seen it. It was Challenge Seekers Masterclass. So what we did is instead of just like when someone joins the Challenge Seekers, like giving them, here's the share funnel and you can use to build the share funnel. So, yeah, this is going to be an example walkthrough of just creating the framework itself. As I said, it's a super simple process. You basically can log into your account and click create framework. You're going to go through here, simply name your framework. You're going to decide what's going to be included in it, right? Like do you have a theme that you're going to share as part of your framework? Do you have funnels? Do you have courses? In Pedro's example, we shared an entire course. All the modules, all the lessons, structured with the right timing, the drip delays, everything that you would want to do for his best practices. You can include the store, which means you can literally include a fully baked e-commerce style store and the products that go along with it. So if you're teaching people anything related to physical product fulfillment, you could use something such as Zindrop and you could actually share all that stuff over into their accounts automatically and give them a jump off spot. Publish the changes when you're ready and all this is going to do is spit you out a sharing URL. As soon as it's done, it's a link you give to people. That's it. Simple as that. The process is super easy. People click the link, install it into their account, installs all the frameworks. If they want to turn it into a brand new workspace, they get presented with that option. If they want to actually add it to an existing business that they have, they get presented with that option. And if someone is actually using it to create a new account, a new business, a new workspace, that actually results in you guys getting affiliate commissions, which is a whole other aspect to this. You can obviously license things, as Russell was saying, in that example and actually just sell the framework contents directly. In that sense. Or you can go out there and give this away completely. Give the IP away, give the ideas away, and get affiliate commissions from us in return for people signing up and using your frameworks.
